hypothesis: If XGBoost models trained to predict the difference between GFN1-xTB and DFT band gaps (∆-learning) using the selected ST-135/RAC/APRDF descriptors outperform direct XGBoost models trained to predict DFT band gaps and achieve comparable or better mean absolute error than fine-tuned deep learning models (MOFTransformer and CGCNN), then using GFN1-xTB as a baseline with ∆-learning provides a more accurate and data-efficient approach to predict MOF DFT band gaps because the semi-empirical method captures systematic electronic structure errors that the ML model can efficiently correct.
